title: Lomatium lithosolamans
taxonomy: Lomatium lithosolamans is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada. The plant is classified in the genus Lomatium and the species L. lithosolamans. Its classification further includes the kingdom Plantae, and the class Magnoliopsida. Within the plant family Apiaceae, it is part of the tribe Selineae.

intro: Lomatium lithospermum (also known as Lomatium lithosolamans) is a perennial plant species native to the western United States, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Lomatium lithospermum include alkaloids, coumarins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its pharmacological properties. Lomatium lithospermum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, often in combination with other medicinal plants, to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
